name: omarchy_fingerprint_&_fido2_authentication
content:
Fingerprint authentication 
A ot of aptops, ike my beoved Framework 13, come with a fingerprint sensor to do
authentication. You can use this with Omarchy by running Setup > Security > Fingerprint
in the Omarchy menu (Super + Alt + Space). That' insta the fingerprint package,
coect your print, verify it, and you' be set to go using your fingerprint to unock from the
ock screen (which you can trigger with Super + Escape ), enter sudo mode, and authorize
system prompts.
If you've setup fingerprint authentication, but then need to work on an externa keyboard
that doesn't have it, just hit CTRL + C , when you're prompted for your fingerprint during
sudo .
You can remove the fingerprint authentication under Remove > Fingerprint in the Omarchy
menu.
Both adding and removing fingerprint authentication is aso avaiabe through the Omarchy
TUI under the Setup menu.
Fido2 authentication 
If you're using a Fido2 device, you can set it up for sudo  authentication using Setup >
Security > Fido2 in the Omarchy menu (Super + Alt + Space). It's ony for sudo , though,
not unocking your computer.
You can remove the fido2 authentication under Remove > Fido2 in the Omarchy menu.
Fingerprint & Fido2 authentication · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/77/fingerprint-fido2-aut...
